scope_note: This rerun focuses specifically on validating the newly generated deterministic seed (data/audits/openserp-serverless-state-seed.sql) against real PostgreSQL with real Supabase-compatible roles. Properties already fully validated in the first Gate B run (data/audits/openserp-serverless-state-gate-b.json) -- rotation/budget persistence across invocations, run-lock concurrency, CAPTCHA suspension persistence, failed-transaction rollback, absence of source_offer_observations/property_clusters -- are unaffected by the seed addition (same schema) and are not re-run here to avoid redundant work.
engine: PostgreSQL 18.2 (native Windows binary, local, disposable data directory, ephemeral, dropped after the gate)

test_1_seed_apply_first_time.expected: 2718 rotation rows, 3 budget rows inserted, matching data/audits/openserp-serverless-state-seed.json manifest exactly

test_2_seed_idempotent_reapply.expected: re-applying the same seed SQL a second time inserts 0 new rows, no errors

test_3_seed_never_overwrites_existing_hi.expected: a manually-modified row (successful_run_count=5) survives a further seed re-apply untouched
test_3_seed_never_overwrites_existing_hi.actual: successful_run_count still 5 after re-applying the seed a third time

test_5_rls_anon_insert_rejected.actual: ERROR: new row violates row-level security policy for table "openserp_engine_budget_state"
